Reverted 2 edits by [[Special:Contributions/Anggona Era|Anggona Era]] ([[User talk:Anggona Era|talk]]): [[MOS:ENGVAR]]

The short description should use the sport's formal name. It's not really an ENGVAR issue because it's not part of the article's text, but it's also misleading and wrong to insist on the short form "football" because that word is ambiguous and can refer to multiple different things (soccer, football, Aussie rules, etc.).
/* Price gouging */I don't really like this section in general anyways, but at least now it's cleaned up a bit.
/* Ticket prices */ "lottery system" is not a compound and doesn’t need a hyphen. Also, I think "singular" isn’t what was meant.
Replace "game(s)" with "match(es)" throughout, with the one Canadian football reference left as "game"
/* Ticket prices */ [[MOS:$]]. I’m not sure "US" is needed here per that same guidance, but I’ve left it for now.
Do not change to 'association football' per [[MOS:ENGVAR]]
Italy should be singular, added link, and only website name is needed, if it's the same as Publisher.
Controversies should be plural, like how it was a few months ago.
Added missing authors and fixed references
/* Absentees */ please respect [[MOS:ENGVAR]]. The correct spelling in American English is "playoff," not "play-off."
Removing reference that doesn't address Italy missing their third consecutive World Cup.
They weren't really "other expansion formats" since they never materialized
